The fallback path triggers when `trySonicQuery()` returns an empty array OR when Sonic throws. Confirmed: the code path exists and the return shape tags `source: 'sqlite' | 'sonic+sqlite'`. No fix needed; closing observation only — caller needs to actually inspect `source` to know which path served the query, and today no consumer does.

userNote: No tests for the lifecycle. start/stop/restart/adopt-external/health-check-timeout paths are exercised only by the bible's smoke target (`node start.js /tmp/st8-smoke-target`). Need unit tests at minimum for: (a) binary_missing graceful degrade, (b) adopt-if-already-running (mock TCP listener on 1491), (c) health-check timeout → SIGTERM cleanup, (d) idempotent re-start. Lifecycle bugs here cascade into every search query.
actionsTaken: Created tests/features/search/sonic-daemon-lifecycle.test.js — 10 tests covering: (a) binary_missing graceful degrade (renames real binary aside, asserts reason='binary_missing' + lastError); (b) adopt-if-already-running via a real net.createServer() bound to 127.0.0.1:1491 (skips if port is externally in use — no false-positive); (c-d) idempotent re-start via the adopt path; (e) async stop() — invariant probes that the spin-wait `while (... Date.now() ... killed)` regex is ABSENT from sonic-daemon.js and that child.once('exit') is PRESENT (Wave 5B ticket 10 ratchet); (f) panic-recovery cap test using schedulePanicRestart() — exercises MAX_PANIC_RESTARTS=3, asserts the 4th call returns null (Wave 5B ticket 5); (g) Sonic-indexer dedup tracker GC across reindex covering clearProjectFromTracker + resetTracker (Wave 5B ticket 7). Plus a getStatus shape assertion. All tests use explicit timeouts; no unbounded awaits. New exports added to sonic-daemon.js: schedulePanicRestart, _resetPanicState, _getState (test seams, prefixed _ to mark internal). Suite: 261 → 271. Mutation probe (bumping MAX_PANIC_RESTARTS to 999): tests 6 + 7 failed; cap-and-backoff probes have real teeth. Commit dd272ed.
residualConcerns: Health-check-timeout path (HEALTH_CHECK_MAX_MS=5000) is NOT directly covered — exercising it would require spawning a real subprocess that opens stdio but never binds 1491, which is too brittle for unit scope. The adopt + binary_missing tests cover the alternate degrade paths, and the health_check_failed reason code is documented in sonic-daemon.js. A future smoke-target integration test (out of unit scope) could exercise it.

userNote: Line 159: `fs.chmodSync(SONIC_BINARY, 0o755)` runs on every `start()`. The binary only needs the executable bit set once (post-clone or post-CI-extract). Re-chmoding is harmless but wasteful and could mask permission issues if someone tightens perms intentionally. Worth one-shotting (check first, only chmod if not executable) or moving to a post-install hook.
actionsTaken: Moved chmod to module-load via ensureBinaryExecutable(). Idempotent: stat-checks owner +x bit and only chmods if missing; sets a module-scoped flag so re-starts skip the syscall. start() still calls the helper as a safety net for late-materialized binaries. Verified binary stays -rwxr-xr-x. Commit 76f2dca.
reviewNote: ensureBinaryExecutable() runs once at module load, gated by _binaryEnsuredExecutable flag + owner-+x bit check (mode & 0o100). start() calls it again as a late-materialization safety net but the flag still short-circuits. Errors caught non-fatal. `ls -la docs/Sonic/sonic` confirms -rwxr-xr-x preserved.

userNote: Upstream Sonic v1.4.9 panics on broken-pipe — smoke tests caught the Rust daemon dying when a client disconnects uncleanly (mid-PUSH, lost socket, etc.). This is an upstream issue. Mitigation today is that sonic-daemon's adopt-if-already-running path would re-attach after a systemd restart, but inside st8 a panicked Sonic leaves the daemon in `available: false` until next `start()`. Worth either: (a) auto-restart on unexpected child exit, (b) wrapping the sonic-client's send/recv to retry on ECONNRESET, or (c) filing an upstream issue with a repro.
actionsTaken: Picked (a) AND (b) per prompt guidance — defense in depth, tested independently. (a) sonic-daemon.js: when the spawned child emits 'exit' AND `_intentionalStop` is false, schedulePanicRestart() fires with exponential backoff [1s, 5s, 30s] capped at MAX_PANIC_RESTARTS=3. After the cap, _state.panicGaveUp=true and the daemon stays unavailable until a manual start() call (which resets the counter via _state.panicRestartCount=0). stop() sets _intentionalStop=true before SIGTERM so our own kills don't trigger panic recovery. setTimeout.unref() keeps the recovery timer from blocking process exit. Logged with attempt counter: '[sonic-daemon] Sonic exited unexpectedly (likely broken-pipe panic). Attempt N/3 after Xms backoff.' Give-up log: '[sonic-daemon] Sonic panicked N times; giving up. Running in SQLite-only mode.' (b) sonic-client.js: push() catch block now matches /EPIPE|ECONNRESET|Connection lost|Not connected/i and force-disconnects the ingestChannel so the next push attempts a clean reconnect rather than reusing a half-dead handle. QUIT-on-disconnect was already wired (line 110) so clean close was already in place — this hardens the unclean-close case. Test coverage: tests/features/search/sonic-daemon-lifecycle.test.js MAX_PANIC_RESTARTS cap test (4th schedulePanicRestart returns null) + backoff-array invariant test. Mutation probe (cap=999): both tests failed; teeth confirmed. Commit 3f55bb5.
residualConcerns: Reconnect-on-broken-pipe inside push() force-disconnects the channel but does NOT auto-reconnect-and-retry the same PUSH — the caller (sonic-indexer) sees false return and loses that record. A future enhancement could retry once with fresh connect; today the indexer's `incrementalIndex` reindex pass would catch the gap on the next pass. Upstream Rust fix (option c) is out of scope (multi-day Rust work).

userNote: `APP_ID = 'com.scaffolder.app'` is the MAESTRO-era identifier. Founder decision needed: should st8 (a) keep `com.scaffolder.app` because the directory IS the maestro exchange-surface and they're meant to share it, (b) switch to `com.st8.app` and break the bridge (which doesn't exist yet anyway), or (c) make APP_ID configurable so st8-standalone deployments don't collide with a maestro install. This is the explicit deferred-by-founder call from the bible.
actionsTaken: Picked (b) — renamed APP_ID in src/shared/utils/ground-plane.js from 'com.scaffolder.app' to 'com.st8.app'. Founder guidance from prompt: 'st8 is its own thing; maestro should not influence any decisions for st8.' The cross-tool ground-plane bridge to MAESTRO was never built (verified via grep: no st8 code reads or writes the ground-plane directory) and roadmap P1 'Restore the founder-intended exchange surface' documents the future bridge as its own ticket — that future bridge will design its own exchange-channel rather than implicitly co-share APP_ID. Grep audit: found 1 live-code reference to 'com.scaffolder.app' in src/ (ground-plane.js line 58, now updated). src/core/database/graph-persister.js:96 ALSO contains 'com.scaffolder.app' in getSharedDatabasePath(), but this is intentionally retained — it points at scaffolder_data.sqlite (used by InsightStore, compiled-from-TS) and renaming the path would orphan existing on-disk insight data on every developer machine. Added an explanatory comment block at graph-persister.js linking ticket 6 + flagging the migration as a future ticket. Other 'com.scaffolder.app' refs are all in docs/, .planning/ (planning archives), and st8_bible.md — historical/reference, no behavioral effect. Total: 1 live ref changed (ground-plane), 1 live ref documented-and-deferred (graph-persister). Commit cfb0f2e.
residualConcerns: graph-persister.js getSharedDatabasePath() still uses 'com.scaffolder.app' for the InsightStore SQLite path. A clean break would require a migration step (read old path, copy rows into new path, swap pointers) AND a touch to compiled-from-TS InsightStore — both out of scope for ticket 6 which is scoped to the APP_ID identifier. Tracked as a future migration ticket (no roadmap entry created — this is a single, narrow follow-up rather than a P1/P2 capability).

userNote: Sonic store is per-target (`<targetDir>/.st8/sonic-store/`). What happens on re-index? Sonic's FST consolidates periodically (`consolidate_after = 180` in sonic.cfg), but the RocksDB KV grows indefinitely until manually compacted — old `node_*`/`file_*` IDs that no longer exist in the graph are never popped. No GC path. Long-running projects will see the on-disk store accumulate. Decide: (a) full POP+repush on every index, (b) deletion of `.st8/sonic-store/` before each re-index, (c) leave it (Sonic is small enough — 21 MB / 1M objects — that even 10x growth is OK), (d) add a `--rebuild-sonic` CLI flag.
actionsTaken: AUDIT — the userNote's premise ('no GC path; old IDs never popped') was incorrect. Code-level findings from src/features/search/sonic-indexer.js: (1) reindexProject(projectId) at line 195 calls `this.client.flush(COLLECTION, projectId)` BEFORE re-pushing the graph — Sonic FLUSHB destroys the entire per-project bucket. Old IDs ARE popped. (2) incrementalIndex(changes, ...) iterates `client.flushObject(...)` for prior fingerprints of each add/modify/delete BEFORE re-pushing the new state — per-record GC is wired. (3) `clearProjectFromTracker(projectId)` clears the session-scoped dedup set so re-runs don't accumulate cross-pass dedup state. Re-index has destructive-flush + re-push semantics at the bucket level. Documented audit findings as a top-of-file JSDoc block in sonic-daemon.js (the userNote was filed against sonic-daemon.js so audit lives there for grep-discoverability), naming the three GC mechanisms by line number. Test coverage: tests/features/search/sonic-daemon-lifecycle.test.js exercises both clearProjectFromTracker (asserts per-project-prefix removal, leaves other-project entries intact) and resetTracker (asserts full clear) on a real SonicIndexer instance with a stub client. Mutation probe: temporarily made clearProjectFromTracker a no-op — tracker GC test failed; tests have teeth. Commit 8175299.
residualConcerns: Sonic's RocksDB KV may retain tombstoned word→ID mappings until SST compaction (Sonic-internal, bounded by sonic.cfg's consolidate_after=180s). No code change required — the on-disk store plateaus rather than growing unboundedly. P3 roadmap entry 'Sonic store GC / per-target rebuild' (already in docs/_pending-roadmap/sonic-and-search.md) captures the optional --rebuild-sonic CLI flag for ops convenience; not blocking.

userNote: No CI / lint check that the canonical `docs/Sonic/sonic.cfg` stays in sync with what `sonic-daemon.js` expects. The daemon hardcodes: port 1491, IPv4 host, `auth_password` not read (relies on MAESTRO's `maestro_scaffolder_key`), `${SONIC_STORE_PATH}` substitution. If MAESTRO bumps the config (renames sections, changes port, removes the env-var substitution), the daemon will spawn Sonic with a config it can't query. Need either: (a) a parse-and-validate step in the daemon's start path, (b) a CI assertion that key fields exist, or (c) explicit version-pinning of the upstream sonic.cfg.
actionsTaken: Picked option (a) — added validateSonicConfig() to sonic-daemon.js that runs before spawn. Checks required sections, declared port matches SONIC_PORT, auth_password key present, ${SONIC_STORE_PATH} substitution appears at least twice (kv + fst). On failure, start() returns the specific reason and falls through to SQLite-only mode. 9 unit tests cover canonical-cfg pass + each synthesized failure mode. Commit 4154b85.
reviewNote: validateSonicConfig() inspects the five required sections, parses inet host:port and compares to SONIC_PORT (1491), confirms auth_password presence, and counts ${SONIC_STORE_PATH} substitutions (≥2). Distinct reason codes (config_read_failed/missing_sections/missing_inet/inet_unparseable/port_mismatch/missing_auth_password/missing_store_path_subst) bubble through start()'s graceful-degrade. Targeted run: 9 pass / 0 fail.

userNote: auth_password in sonic.cfg is `maestro_scaffolder_key` — hardcoded and shared. Anyone with localhost access can hit Sonic. For local-dev this is fine; for any deployment that exposes Sonic beyond loopback (which the daemon doesn't do today, but a future systemd unit might) this is a credential leak. Worth either: (a) generating a per-target random password and patching both sonic.cfg and the client config at materialize-time, (b) keeping the shared key but documenting the loopback-only contract explicitly, or (c) deferring until a deployment story exists.
actionsTaken: Picked option (a), mirroring Wave 2C .st8/server.secret pattern. New ensureSonicPassword(targetDir) writes .st8/sonic.password (mode 0600, 32 random bytes hex) on first start. Runtime cfg materialization replaces auth_password in the patched copy; sonic-daemon then calls sonicClient.setPassword() (new method) so the singleton's three channels use the new key on next connect. Canonical docs/Sonic/sonic.cfg untouched. Graceful degrade to canonical key on filesystem error. 7 unit tests cover file creation, idempotency, mode 0600, regenerate-on-truncation, setPassword across channels, invalid-input rejection, runtime-cfg patching. Commit 86218e1.
reviewNote: Independent probe (ensureSonicPassword on a fresh /tmp dir): 64 hex chars, file mode 600. setPassword updates this.password + searchChannel/ingestChannel/controlChannel option fields atomically; invalid input early-return preserves existing pw. start() requires sonic-client and invokes setPassword inside try/catch — graceful degrade preserved. Targeted run: 7 pass / 0 fail. MUTATION PROBE: stubbed setPassword body with early-return → 'updates all three channels' test failed (6/7). Restored cleanly. Tests have real teeth.

userNote: `stop()` uses a synchronous spin-wait (`while (Date.now() - start < SHUTDOWN_GRACE_MS && !child.killed)`) on lines 260-263. This burns 1.5 s of CPU in a tight loop on every shutdown and blocks the event loop. Should be either an async setTimeout + child.once('exit') race, or skip the grace window entirely (SIGTERM then SIGKILL immediately if still alive on next tick).
actionsTaken: Replaced the sync spin-wait with an async setTimeout + child.once('exit') race. stop() now returns a Promise that resolves when the child has exited (either via clean SIGTERM response OR via the SHUTDOWN_GRACE_MS-deadline SIGKILL). Implementation: SIGTERM is sent; we attach child.once('exit', finish) and start a setTimeout(finish, SHUTDOWN_GRACE_MS); whichever fires first calls finish() which is idempotent via a `settled` latch, clears the timer, removes the exit listener, sends SIGKILL only if the child is still alive (child.exitCode === null), nulls _state.process, and resolves. setTimeout.unref() prevents the grace timer from holding the event loop alive when nothing else is pending. Edge case: if the child already exited between our `if (child.killed)` check and the listener attach (race), we re-check child.exitCode !== null after attach and call finish() synchronously. Verification: grep `while.*killed` against the post-change source returns ZERO matches in sonic-daemon.js — the spin-wait is gone. Tests added in lifecycle suite: (1) stop() returns a thenable and resolves in <200ms when no child is present; (2) source-invariant probe — the regex `/while\s*\([^)]*Date\.now[^)]*killed/` must NOT match in sonic-daemon.js (Wave 5B ratchet against regression) AND `child.once('exit'` must be present. Commit 8df4b9b.
residualConcerns: Existing internal callers of stop() (exit handlers in installExitHandlers()) fire-and-forget the promise. That's fine because (a) the SIGTERM goes through synchronously and (b) the process is exiting anyway. No behavioral regression; documented in the function's JSDoc.
